DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-04-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ELINA TODOROV'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF HUSAINS COMPLIANCE WITH RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF SYED HUSAIN, SET ONE, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. IN EACH RESPONSE TO REQUESTS 1-13, HUSAIN MAKES BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS AND THEN SAYS SUBJECT TO AND WITHOUT WAIVING THE OBJECTIONS, HE WILL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE NONPRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. HUSAIN'S ATTORNEY SAYS HUSAIN HAS PRODUCED ALL NONPRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS BUT HUSAIN HAS NOT YET SAID THAT UNDER OATH. IF THERE ARE ANY NONPRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS THAT HUSAIN HAS NOT YET PRODUCED, HE SHALL DO SO. ONCE HUSAIN HAS PRODUCED ALL NONPRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, HE WILL AMEND HIS RESPONSES TO REQUESTS 1-13 TO STATE THAT HE HAS PRODUCED ALL NONPRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. IN EACH RESPONSE, HE SHALL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE REQUEST BY BATES NUMBER. PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY TO PREPARE THE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURES IN RULE 3.1312 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-04-2020 HEARING RE: AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT ELINA EMILY TODOROVS FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANTS COUNSEL, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. 	. TODOROV SHALL IDENTIFY BY BATES-NUMBERS THE SPECIFIC INVOICES, BILL, RECEIPTS AND CORRESPONDENCES IDENTIFIED IN HER RESPONSE TO THIS INTERROGATORY. #25. TODOROV SHALL STATE THE NAME, ADDRESS, AND TELEPHONE NUMBER OF THE "COWORKER" WHO PURCHASED THE TICKETS. #41. IT IS NOT CLEAR IF TODOROV'S ANSWER IS CONTRADICTORY. SHE MIGHT BE SAYING THAT SHE HAD NO COMMUNICATIONS TO RECOVER FURNITURE AND THERE IS CORRESPONDENCE THAT SHOWS SHE DIDN'T SEEK TO RECOVER FURNITURE. OR THE ANSWER MIGHT BE CONTRADICTORY. TODOROV IS ORDERED TO CLARIFY THE ANSWER. IF SHE HAD NO COMMUNICATIONS SEEKING TO RECOVER FURNITURE, THEN SHE SHOULD DELETE THE SECOND SENTENCE OF THE INTERROGATORY ANSWER. IF SHE HAD COMMUNICATIONS TO RECOVER FURNITURE, THEN SHE SHOULD DELETE THE FIRST SENTENCE OF THE INTERROGATORY ANSWER AND IDENTIFY BY BATES NUMBER THE CORRESPONDENCES REFERRED TO IN HER SECOND SENTENCE. PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY TO PREPARE THE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURES IN RULE 3.1312 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELINA TODOROV'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IN THE FORM OF COSTS, ATTORNEYS FEES AND DISMISSAL FOR ACTIONS AND TACTICS THAT ARE FRIVOLOUS, HARASSING AND IN BAD FAITH. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE 21-DAY SAFE HARBOR PROVISION. (CODE CIV. PROC. 128.5(F)(1)(B); SEE NUTRITION DISTRIBUTION, LLC V. SOUTHERN SARMS, INC. (2018) 20 CAL.APP.5TH 117, 129-130.) THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION DIRECTS PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL TO SHOW CAUSE WHY IT SHOULD NOT DIRECT THEM TO PAY MONETARY AND OTHER SANCTIONS UNLESS, WITHIN 21 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE, THE COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. 128.5(F)(1)(D), 128.5(F)(2)(B).) THE GROUND FOR THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS THAT PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL HAVE FILED AND CONTINUED TO PROSECUTE A FRIVOLOUS COMPLAINT. (SEE CIV. CODE 43.4, 43.5; ASKEW V. ASKEW (1994) 22 CAL.APP.4TH 942.) A HEARING ON THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WILL BE HELD ON JULY 22, 2020 AT 9:30 A.M. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN JULY 17, 2020. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-04-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SYED HUSAIN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. HOWEVER, THE COURT SUBSTITUTES ITS OWN PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER IN PLACE OF HUSAIN'S PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE REASON IS THAT PLAINTIFF IS IN PROPRIA PERSONA SO IN REALITY HUSAIN HAS NO ADVERSARY TRAINED IN LAW TO POINT OUT TO THE COURT THE PROBLEMS, IF ANY, IN HIS PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER. THEREFORE, THE COURT PREFERS TO START WITH A PROTECTIVE ORDER THAT IT ALREADY KNOWS AND TRUSTS RATHER THAN TRY TO FIGURE OUT WHAT PROBLEMS, IF ANY, THERE MIGHT BE WITH HUSAIN'S PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE PARTIES SHALL FILE WITH THE COURT BY NOVEMBER 25, 2020, ANY OBJECTIONS OR PROPOSED CHANGES TO THE COURT'S PROPOSED PROTECTIVE ORDER AND THE REASONS THEREFOR. PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY TO PREPARE THE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURES IN RULE 3.1312 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-04-2020 HEARING RE: SECOND AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT ELINA EMILY TODOROVS RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANTS COUNSEL, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TODOROV'S RESPONSE TO EACH REQUEST WAS TO MAKE BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS AND THEN SAY SUBJECT TO AND WITHOUT WAIVING THE OBJECTIONS, SHE WILL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE NONPRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. HUSAIN SEEMS TO BE CONCERNED THAT PERHAPS SHE WITHHELD DOCUMENTS BASED ON HER BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS. TO FORESTALL THIS CONCERN, TODOROV SHALL AMEND HER RESPONSES TO STATE THAT SHE HAS PRODUCED ALL RESPONSIVE NONPRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, IN EACH RESPONSE, SHE SHALL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO THE REQUEST BY BATES NUMBER. SHE WILL ALSO PRODUCE ANY NONPRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS SHE HAS NOT YET PRODUCED. PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY TO PREPARE THE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROCEDURES IN RULE 3.1312 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SYED HUSAIN's Motion For Protective Order And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Granted. However, the court substitutes its own proposed protective order in place of Husain's proposed protective order. The reason is that plaintiff is in propria persona so in reality Husain has no adversary trained in law to point out to the court the problems, if any, in his proposed protective order. Therefore, the court prefers to start with a protective order that it already knows and trusts rather than try to figure out what problems, if any, there might be with Husain's proposed protective order. The parties shall file with the court by November 25, 2020, any objections or proposed changes to the court's proposed protective order and the reasons therefor.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 128.5
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SYED HUSAIN's Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 128.5.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Syed Husain's motion for sanctions against defendant and defendant's counsel is denied. Plaintiff does not establish that defendant's motion for sanctions was filed with subjective bad faith, was frivolous, or was intended to cause delay. (See In re Marriage of Sahafzadeh-Taeb & Taeb (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 124, 135; Dolan v. Buena Engineers, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1500, 1505.) Neither defendant's failure to comply with the 21-day safe harbor period in filing her motion nor the fact that the court overruled defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint provides grounds for an award of sanctions. Likewise, defendant's request for sanctions in opposing this motion is denied. The court disapproves the parties' repeated reflexive requests for sanctions against each other, and will not tolerate a continuation of such conduct. Whatever personal animosity may exist between the parties, counsel are expected to conduct themselves professionally and focus on the merits of the claims and defenses.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Seith Weiner, Esq. 925-487-5607 for Plaintiff.
Joshua Davis, 415-922-5454 for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ELINA TODOROV's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Elina Todorov's demurrers to the first amended complaint (FAC) are overruled.

Demurrers 1-5 center on the "anti-heart balm" statute: Civil Code Sec. 43.5, which bars causes of action for "alienation of affection." However, the FAC pleads that defendant and plaintiff were friends for 14 years (dating for five months of that) and that defendant owes for concert tickets, travel expenses, storage, failure to return a key and credit cards and the like. Defendant cites no authority for the notion that Civil Code Sec. 43.5 bars all suits between former friends who ever dated, however briefly.

Defendant's other demurrer couples the heart-balm statute with the sham pleading doctrine. This is an effort to saddle plaintiff with his original complaint, which did run afoul of the statute. But the doctrine's purpose "is to prevent [an] amended pleading which is only a sham, when it is apparent that no cause of action can be stated truthfully." (JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Ward (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 678, 690.) Here, the FAC re-writes the original complaint to state facially legitimate causes of action rather than "heart balm."

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Order To Show Cause (For Plaintiff And His Counsel As To Why It Should Not Pay Monetary Sanctions)

The order to show cause is discharged. Plaintiff concedes that his original complaint was "untenable" and "did arguably run afoul of the anti-heart-balm laws insofar as it sought redress for the breach of 'Defendant's agreement to continue with her relationship with Plaintiff for at least one year without disappearing or otherwise terminating the relationship.'" The Court expresses no view as to whether the verified first amended complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. This order is without prejudice to defendant filing a demurrer or other responsive pleading.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions In The Form Of Costs, Attorneys Fees And Dismissal For Actions And Tactics That Are Frivolous, Harassing And In Bad Faith
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ELINA TODOROV's Motion For Sanctions In The Form Of Costs, Attorneys Fees And Dismissal For Actions And Tactics That Are Frivolous, Harassing And In Bad Faith.

Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied because defendant did not comply with the 21-day safe harbor provision. (Code Civ. Proc. 128.5(f)(1)(b); see Nutrition Distribution, LLC v. Southern Sarms, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 117, 129-130.)

The Court on its own motion directs plaintiff and his counsel to show cause why it should not direct them to pay monetary and other sanctions unless, within 21 days of service of the order to show cause, the complaint is dismissed with prejudice. (See Code Civ. Proc. 128.5(f)(1)(D), 128.5(f)(2)(B).) The ground for the order to show cause is that plaintiff and his counsel have filed and continued to prosecute a frivolous complaint. (See Civ. Code 43.4, 43.5; Askew v. Askew (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 942.) A hearing on the order to show cause will be held on July 22, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. Plaintiff's response to the order to show cause shall be filed and served no later than July 17, 2020.

The Court will prepare an order re: Order to Show Cause.
